commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds LINE outbound media `HEAD` size prechecks, cap constants, URL redaction, soft-fail logging, and focused tests for image, video, audio, and preview media sends.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main sends LINE media after URL validation without a size precheck, and the LINE reference defines smaller preview caps that the PR is trying to enforce.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can update the two implicit-preview branches and add focused tests without a product decision.

target.title: fix(line): precheck outbound LINE media size
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `LINE_OUTBOUND_MEDIA_MAX_BYTES` (image `originalContentUrl` 10 MB,
  video / audio `originalContentUrl` 200 MB, `previewImageUrl` 1 MB;
  verified against the LINE Messaging API reference on 2026-05-20) and
  `precheckLineOutboundMediaSize` in
  `extensions/line/src/outbound-media.ts`. The helper issues a HEAD via
  `fetchWithSsrFGuard` (5 s, `requireHttps`, `mode: "strict"`, reusing
  the existing `LINE_OUTBOUND_MEDIA_SSRF_POLICY` —
  `allowPrivateNetwork: false`).
- Hard-fail on `200/206` with `Content-Length > cap`. Soft-fail
  (`logVerbose` + pass through) on probe error, non-2xx status, absent
  / malformed / negative `Content-Length`. Logs strip the query string.
- Wire the precheck next to every existing `validateLineMediaUrl` site:
  `resolveLineOutboundMedia`, the video / audio / image branches in
  `sendMessageLine`, and `pushImageMessage`. `previewImageUrl` is
  always checked against the 1 MB preview cap (it is strictly smaller
  than the `originalContentUrl` cap, so LINE rejects oversized previews
  even when the original would pass).
- Dedupe: when `previewImageUrl === mediaUrl` (or the implicit fallback
  produces an equal URL), issue a single HEAD probe and evaluate it
  against the **stricter** preview cap — one network round-trip, both
  LINE-side validations covered.

## Behavior change
Outbound LINE media sends whose host reveals a body larger than the
LINE per-role cap now fail locally with a descriptive error. Today the
LINE platform fetches the URL asynchronously after our `pushMessage` /
`replyMessage` call returns, so an oversize file produces no synchronous
error on the caller side. Hosts that don't expose `Content-Length` keep
working unchanged — the precheck logs and passes through.

## Notes
- The adapter (`extensions/line/src/outbound.ts`, untouched by this
  PR) chains `resolveLineOutboundMedia` → `sendMessageLine`. Both
  layers now precheck, so the kind-aware adapter path issues two
  HEAD probes per media URL — the same shape as the existing
  `validateLineMediaUrl` duplication. Acceptable for the
  user-driven outbound media path; a future PR can collapse it once
  `outbound.ts` is in scope.
- Format / dimensions / duration validation is out of scope. Only
  size is gated.

## Non-Scope
- No changes to `src/media/**` or `src/plugin-sdk/**`. Independent
  of #76566 (MIME helpers — no file overlap).
- No new account-level config knob.

## Real behavior proof

### HEAD identity at submission

- Branch: `pr/line-media-size-precheck`
- Submitted HEAD: rebased onto current `upstream/main` (see "Rebase note" below)
- Commits on top of base (2):
  - `fix(line): enforce LINE preview image size cap` (preview-cap)
  - `fix(line): precheck outbound media size` (precheck)

### Rebase note

The branch was rebased onto `upstream/main` immediately before
submission. The rebase brought 22 upstream commits onto the base.
None of those upstream commits touched the four files in this PR
(`extensions/line/src/outbound-media.ts`, `outbound-media.test.ts`,
`send.ts`, `send.test.ts`); `git log <pre-rebase-HEAD>..upstream/main
-- <those files>` is empty. The post-rebase blob hashes for the four
target files are byte-identical to the pre-rebase blobs (verified
via `git rev-parse <SHA>:<file>` equality). Therefore the live LINE
Messaging API proof captured against the pre-rebase HEAD continues
to hold for the submitted HEAD — the production code path under
test is the same source tree.

### Exact cap values (verified against developers.line.biz/en/reference/messaging-api/ on 2026-05-20)

| Role | Cap (bytes) |
|---|---|
| image `originalContentUrl` | 10 485 760 |
| video `originalContentUrl` | 209 715 200 |
| audio `originalContentUrl` | 209 715 200 |
| `previewImageUrl` (image / video) | 1 048 576 |

### Live proof (LINE Messaging API)

Driver imports `dist/extensions/line/runtime-api.js` from the rebuilt
worktree, so the live cases exercise the production precheck path
including the preview-cap wiring.

| Case | What | Result |
|---|---|---|
| A | `sendMessageLine` image, `previewImageUrl === mediaUrl`, file = 3 116 B | LINE app received the message; fixture origin log shows 1 HEAD (equal-URL dedupe) then LINE-side GET. |
| B | `sendMessageLine` image, `originalContentUrl` 14 545 753 B (>10 MiB) + valid small preview | Local `Error: LINE image media must be ≤10485760 bytes (got 14545753 bytes from <FIXTURE_HOST>/image-overcap.png)`; `client.pushMessage` never invoked. Origin log shows 1 HEAD on the over-cap original (no preview HEAD, no LINE-side GET). |
| C | `sendMessageLine` image, valid small `originalContentUrl` + `previewImageUrl` = 1 489 176 B (between 1 MiB and 10 MiB) | Local `Error: LINE preview media must be ≤1048576 bytes (got 1489176 bytes from <FIXTURE_HOST>/mid-preview-overcap.jpg)`; `client.pushMessage` never invoked. **This is the key proof for the preview-cap commit.** |
| D | `sendMessageLine` image, `previewImageUrl === mediaUrl`, shared file = 1 489 176 B | Local `Error: LINE preview media must be ≤1048576 bytes ...`; exactly **1 HEAD** for the shared URL (dedupe preserved, stricter cap applied). |
| D-push | `pushImageMessage` with `previewImageUrl === originalContentUrl`, shared file = 1 489 176 B | Same shape as D — 1 HEAD, preview-cap rejection. |

Fixture host was a short-lived Cloudflare quick-tunnel; tunnel torn
down after the proof window. No signed-URL query strings were used.
The named LINE webhook tunnel was not touched.

### Automated proof (at submitted HEAD, post-rebase)

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/line/src/outbound-media.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/line/src/send.test.ts
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 node scripts/check-changed.mjs
```

| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| outbound-media.test.ts | 38 / 38 passed |
| send.test.ts | 18 / 18 passed |
| check:changed | exit 0 (typecheck, oxlint 0 warn / 0 err, runtime import cycles 0, all guards PASS) |

The preview-cap commit adds 4 asserts to `outbound-media.test.ts`
(explicit-preview rejection in `resolveLineOutboundMedia`, equal-URL
shared-file rejection, `kind=preview` unit-level rejection,
`kind=preview` inclusive boundary) and 2 asserts to `send.test.ts`
(`sendMessageLine` image branch preview-cap rejection +
`pushImageMessage` preview-cap rejection). T12 (the pre-existing
oversize image smoke) was widened to accept either the image or
preview rejection message since the implicit `previewImageUrl =
mediaUrl` fallback in the image branch now binds against the
stricter preview cap first.

### What was not tested at the submitted HEAD

- **Live > 200 MB video send.** Phase 0a §6 explicit skip — same
  code path as image; T9 covers the cap math with a synthetic
  `content-length: 209715201` HEAD response.
- **Adapter path (`outbound.ts` → `resolveLineOutboundMedia` →
  `sendMessageLine`) live.** The precheck behavior is identical
  to the direct `sendMessageLine` path proven above; the
  double-HEAD topology for the adapter is disclosed in `## Notes`.
- **Group / room targets live.** Only DM target tested; the precheck
  is target-agnostic.
- **Live soft-fail at the submitted HEAD.** The soft-fail branches
  in `outbound-media.ts` are unchanged across the precheck and
  preview-cap commits; the earlier live soft-fail proof (against a
  no-`Content-Length` `httpbin.org` host) remains valid for the
  submitted HEAD, and T3-T7 unit tests continue to lock the
  soft-fail behavior at 38 / 38 pass.

### Redactions applied

- LINE recipient `userId` substituted by the driver script as
  `<LINE_USER_ID>` (the driver runs `sed -E 's/U[a-f0-9]{32}/<LINE_USER_ID>/g'`
  on stdout / stderr before any file write).
- Cloudflare quick-tunnel subdomain (ephemeral) substituted as
  `<FIXTURE_HOST>` / `<FIXTURE_BASE>` everywhere it appears in this
  PR body and in the captured artifacts.
- `LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN` / `LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET` were sourced
  from a host-side env file (`runtime/secrets/.env`) via
  `set -a; source ...; set +a` and never written to any file in
  the worktree or referenced in any committed file. Token / secret
  values are not present in any PR-visible artifact.
target.author: masatohoshino
